body.u: I left school at the age of seventeen with 3 ‘O’ levels and several C.S.Es and joined a small company as an office boy at the age of eighteen in Sittingbourne. When the company expanded I was transferred to a London branch, which meant leaving home and getting a flat in London with a friend. After six years with the company slowly progressing I became assistant manager. Soon after I bought a house just outside London, then eventually a car. At the age of twenty-six I left the company and became a production manager with a different one. By that time I was thinking about marriage having a car and house and soon hoping to getting a small yacht. I began visiting other countries every year for a holiday, with a group of friends and a girlfriend. At twenty-eight I was offered a job in America with another branch of the same company. I took it. I rented out the house and took a flat in America. After four years then, I came back with thoughts of marriage renewed, still with the same company. At thirty-four I marry a blonde, who is thirty. We live in the house with two cars and after a year she has a baby boy. Eventually when I am thirty-eight we are divorced on grounds of being sick of each other. She takes care of the boy and a car and alimony. After this I put in for my job in America again and spend another six years then during that time I met a Canadian girl and marry her. By this time I would assess myself as a middle-classed manager, divorced, remarryedremarried, three children, (one with my ex-wife and twins by my second). and go to Canada where my mother lives, every year and to England to see my parents every two years. At the age of forty-nine we go back to England and soon after wife has another baby, this is to be the last (we hope). After three years in England my parents die, leaving me with two houses and quite a bit of money. I hear of my first son has gone on to university and his mother has died. I am now fifty-nine and I don’t want to live any longer, so I will leave all my money and property to my wife and children (including the one at university). Retire with them and on my sixtieth birthday, I will kill myself (I hope).
